analysis.reasoning: Core message: An informational announcement that the European Parliament begins oversight of the EU‑UK Trade and Cooperation Agreement with debates in EP committees, inviting people to follow live. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – Not promoted; no claim about EU’s historical success or learning from past. EU2 (EU is united) – Not promoted; no mention of unity or agreement among member states. EU3 (EU useful for its citizens) – Not clearly promoted; while it references parliamentary oversight and committee debates, it does not claim benefits to citizens or highlight practical outcomes improving lives. EU4 (avant‑garde actor) – Not promoted; no pioneering policy or leadership angle. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – Not promoted; no human rights/democracy advocacy message. EU6 (contributes to a better world) – Not promoted; although the EU‑UK deal is external, the tweet does not frame it as contributing to global peace/cooperation or broader well‑being. Conclusion: The tweet is a neutral procedural update and does not promote any listed narrative.
